sections.content: PAN PAN (from French "panne" — breakdown) is the urgency signal used when a vessel or person faces a situation that is serious but not involving grave and imminent danger. Common urgency situations include person overboard, loss of steering, engine failure in a shipping lane, or a medical emergency requiring advice.

The PAN PAN format is: PAN PAN PAN PAN PAN PAN — ALL STATIONS (x3) or specific station — THIS IS — [vessel name x3] — [nature of urgency] — [position] — [assistance required] — [number of persons on board] — [any other useful information] — OVER.

sections.prompt: What signal word indicates an urgency situation?
sections.explanation: PAN PAN is the urgency signal, indicating a serious situation that does not involve immediate danger to the vessel or person. MAYDAY is for distress (grave and imminent danger).
